name: Sauteed Rabbit Loin with Braised Fennel and B
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and pat dry 4 boneless rabbit loins with the flap intact.
2. In a mixing bowl, stir together cold water, red wine vinegar, salt, and black peppercorns.
3. Place rabbit loins in liquid and allow to stand for 1 hour.
4. Remove rabbit from brine and pat dry.
5. In a 12- to 14-inch heavy bottomed sauté pan, heat 4 tablespoons olive oil until smoking.
6. Season rabbit pieces with salt and pepper and sauté until golden brown on both sides, about 6 to 7 minutes. Remove rabbit pieces and set aside.
7. Add fennel, onion, and fennel seeds and cook until soft and lightly browned, about 10 minutes.
8. Add tomato sauce, white wine, and balsamic vinegar and bring to a boil.
9. Place rabbit pieces in the pan and simmer for 15 minutes uncovered.
10. Meanwhile, make salsa verde by soaking bread in white wine vinegar for about 2 minutes, removing bread and squeezing dry, then placing in a food processor with parsley, capers, garlic, spinach, thyme, fennel fronds, and extra virgin olive oil. Blend for 30 seconds until smooth.
11. Remove cooked rabbit pieces from sauce and arrange on a serving platter.
12. Top with fennel sauce mixture, drizzle with salsa verde, and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
